rationale: The revised REFUSE design is coherent and decomposable into three reasonable beads: strict lifecycle/obligation helpers plus the approve gate, documentation/ownership, and focused tests. Consolidated items 1-6 and 8-10 are substantively addressed, and 4gsz is correctly bounded. Approval is blocked by AC runnability. At eb6a2ed1, AC1, AC3, AC5, and AC6's approve commands report '[no tests to run]' yet exit 0; AC2 passes on unrelated existing ApproveImpl tests; AC4 passes on the pre-extension CallOrder test. Thus six AC proofs are non-discriminating. AC7's requested seam-driven cross-package test is also underspecified after approve imports complete's exported predicate: current complete tests use package-private seams, while importing approve from package complete creates a cycle. Finally, the branchless R3 refusal still ends with `mindspec complete <bead>` although the spec admits that command fails before reconciliation; no runnable restoration procedure is specified, and h4n5 is absent from the pinned JSONL. AC10's command checks only two domains, not the asserted exactly-one claimant invariant.
concrete_changes_required: Give AC1-AC6 new, explicitly named test functions/subtests and add existence checks (or another fail-on-zero-match discriminator) so every proof fails at eb6a2ed1; AC4 needs a new discriminator for the extended ordering assertion.

Resolve AC7's package/seam design: split convergence into package-local seam tests plus a named approve post-recovery test, or specify a cycle-free shared helper/test seam; retain an existence check for every named test.

Make branchless R3 recovery truthful and runnable by specifying and testing an exact safe branch-restoration prerequisite before `mindspec complete`, or bring branchless reconciliation into scope; persist the deferred h4n5 issue in pinned project state.
Strengthen AC10 to count `internal/lifecycle/**` claims across every domain OWNERSHIP.yaml and assert exactly one, while separately asserting that claimant is workflow.

findings.note: R1 now uses an error-preserving lifecycle core and refuses on epic lookup, bd-list, or ancestry errors while preserving the fail-open wrapper.
R3 explicitly refuses on missing or corrupt plan bead enumeration and AC6(e) states the falsifier.
The mutation guarantee is correctly narrowed to no close, phase write, merge, or push, with EnsureMigrated disclosed.
Detection is epic-scoped, AC2(c) covers a different-spec orphan, and blp6 remains explicitly deferred.
Scope and AC9 now name the embedded literal and both real materialized skill copies with a refusal-specific discriminator.
AC8 uses the single-line `rule as CompleteBead` removal discriminator; the pinned source contains exactly one occurrence.

AC7 adds the required named-test existence grep and AC10 adds the negative execution discriminator, but AC7's seam/package shape is unresolved and AC10 does not prove exactly one claimant across all domains.
At eb6a2ed1 lifecycle ownership is removed from execution and added only to workflow; repository-wide inspection currently finds one claimant.
The Goal and Non-Goals qualify coverage and disclose both raw-git/no-obligation and cross-spec residuals.
OQ2 rationale, orphans.go bounds, Background ordering, and cited line references are materially corrected.
Background correctly frames 115 as only the impl-approve consumer of the shared 4gsz predicate and leaves CI in 4gsz scope.
Manual branch restoration is acknowledged, but the emitted recovery remains non-runnable for the branchless state and h4n5 is not present in the pinned issues JSONL.
All six proof commands exit 0 at eb6a2ed1 without the required new coverage; four explicitly report no tests to run.
The specified seam-driven complete-to-approve test lacks a cycle-free package and seam arrangement under the proposed approve-to-complete dependency.
